items.question: Is my code safe? Do you see it?
items.answer: IntelligenceX has no backend service. Your code diffs go directly from GitHub Actions to your chosen AI provider (ChatGPT or Copilot). We never see, store, or process your code.

items.question: Why do I use my own ChatGPT or Copilot login?
items.answer: This is the zero-trust design. You authenticate with your own account. No shared API keys, no pooled quotas, no third-party billing. You control the credentials, stored in GitHub Actions secrets that only your workflows can access.

items.question: What is 'Bring Your Own GitHub App'?
items.answer: Instead of trusting our GitHub App, you can create your own App under your organization. This gives you branded bot identity, fine-grained permissions, and full audit control. The CLI wizard supports this flow natively.

items.question: Which AI providers are supported?
items.answer: ChatGPT (native transport via OAuth) and GitHub Copilot (via Copilot CLI). You configure the provider and model in .intelligencex/reviewer.json.

items.question: How do I get started?
items.answer: Run 'intelligencex setup autodetect' first, then use 'intelligencex setup wizard' or 'intelligencex setup web'. Onboarding is path-first (new setup, fix auth, cleanup, maintenance) and runs entirely on your machine.

items.question: Can IntelligenceX detect what path I should take?
items.answer: Yes. Auto-detect runs doctor-style checks (auth/config/GitHub access) and suggests a path before repo selection. It helps you choose between first-time setup, auth refresh, or maintenance.

items.question: How do cleanup and maintenance differ?
items.answer: Cleanup removes workflow/config (and optionally secrets). Maintenance inspects current state first, then guides you to setup, update-secret, or cleanup depending on findings.

items.question: What platforms are supported?
items.answer: Windows, macOS, and Linux. The CLI and library target .NET 8+ and .NET 10+, the PowerShell module supports netstandard2.0, net472, and net8.0. The reviewer runs on any GitHub Actions runner.

items.question: Can I customize the review behavior?
items.answer: Yes. Choose from presets (balanced, picky, security, performance, tests, minimal) or write custom JSON config. Control review mode (inline, summary, hybrid), length, output style, and auto-resolve behavior.

items.question: Is this similar to Claude Code or Codex?
items.answer: The reviewer produces structured, Codex-style output integrated into your GitHub PR workflow. It supports inline comments with code suggestions, summary reviews, and hybrid mode.
